items.docs: Authenticates with the Looker API using client credentials and returns an access token. The client_id and client_secret are generated from the Looker Admin panel under Users > API Keys. The returned access token must be included as a Bearer token in the Authorization header for all subsequent API requests.
Revokes the current API access token, ending the authenticated session. After logout, the token can no longer be used.

params.name: fields
params.description: Comma-separated list of field names to include in the response. Use this to limit response payload and improve performance.

items.docs: Returns a list of all Looks accessible to the authenticated user. Supports filtering by fields to limit the response payload.

params.name: fields
params.description: Comma-separated list of field names to include in the response. Use this to limit response payload and improve performance.

params.name: limit
params.description: Maximum number of results to return

params.name: offset
params.description: Number of results to skip before returning results

params.name: title
params.description: Filter by Look title (case-insensitive match)

params.name: description
params.description: Filter by Look description

params.name: space_id
params.description: Filter by space (folder) ID

params.name: user_id
params.description: Filter by creator user ID

params.name: sorts
params.description: Sort fields (e.g. title asc, created_at desc)

items.docs: Searches for Looks matching the specified criteria. Supports filtering by title, description, space, user, and other attributes. Results are paginated.

params.name: look_id
params.description: The unique numeric ID of the Look

params.name: fields
params.description: Comma-separated list of field names to include in the response. Use this to limit response payload and improve performance.

items.docs: Retrieves a single Look by its ID, including the query definition, visualization configuration, and metadata such as the creator, space, and sharing settings.

params.name: look_id
params.description: The unique numeric ID of the Look

params.name: fields
params.description: Comma-separated list of field names to include in the response. Use this to limit response payload and improve performance.

items.docs: Updates an existing Look. Only the fields provided in the request body are updated; all other fields remain unchanged.

params.name: look_id
params.description: The unique numeric ID of the Look

items.docs: Permanently deletes the specified Look. This action cannot be undone. The Look is moved to trash first if soft delete is enabled.

params.name: look_id
params.description: The unique numeric ID of the Look

params.name: result_format
params.description: Format for the query results

params.name: limit
params.description: Maximum number of results to return

params.name: apply_formatting
params.description: Whether to apply model-specified formatting to results

params.name: apply_vis
params.description: Whether to apply visualization options to results

items.docs: Executes the query associated with a Look and returns the results in the specified format. Supported formats include json, csv, txt, xlsx, html, md, json_label, json_detail, and png.

params.name: fields
params.description: Comma-separated list of field names to include in the response. Use this to limit response payload and improve performance.

items.docs: Returns a list of all dashboards accessible to the authenticated user. User-defined dashboards and LookML dashboards are both included. Use the fields parameter to limit response size.
Creates a new user-defined dashboard. The dashboard can be populated with tiles, filters, and layout settings after creation or as part of the request body.

params.name: fields
params.description: Comma-separated list of field names to include in the response. Use this to limit response payload and improve performance.

params.name: limit
params.description: Maximum number of results to return

params.name: offset
params.description: Number of results to skip before returning results

params.name: title
params.description: Filter by dashboard title

params.name: description
params.description: Filter by dashboard description

params.name: space_id
params.description: Filter by space (folder) ID

params.name: user_id
params.description: Filter by creator user ID

params.name: sorts
params.description: Sort fields

items.docs: Searches for dashboards matching the specified criteria including title, description, space, and creator. Results are paginated.

params.name: dashboard_id
params.description: The dashboard ID. Numeric for user-defined dashboards or model::dashboard_name for LookML dashboards.

params.name: fields
params.description: Comma-separated list of field names to include in the response. Use this to limit response payload and improve performance.

items.docs: Retrieves a single dashboard by its ID, including all dashboard elements (tiles), filters, and layout configuration. For user-defined dashboards the ID is numeric; for LookML dashboards the ID is the model::dashboard_name string.

params.name: dashboard_id
params.description: The dashboard ID. Numeric for user-defined dashboards or model::dashboard_name for LookML dashboards.

items.docs: Updates an existing dashboard. Only the fields provided in the request body are updated. Dashboard elements and filters can be updated through their own dedicated endpoints.

params.name: dashboard_id
params.description: The dashboard ID. Numeric for user-defined dashboards or model::dashboard_name for LookML dashboards.

items.docs: Permanently deletes the specified dashboard and all of its elements, filters, and layout configuration. This action cannot be undone.

params.name: dashboard_id
params.description: The dashboard ID. Numeric for user-defined dashboards or model::dashboard_name for LookML dashboards.

params.name: fields
params.description: Comma-separated list of field names to include in the response. Use this to limit response payload and improve performance.

items.docs: Returns all elements (tiles) for the specified dashboard. Each element contains a query, a Look reference, or a text/markdown content block along with positioning and size information.

params.name: dashboard_id
params.description: The dashboard ID. Numeric for user-defined dashboards or model::dashboard_name for LookML dashboards.

params.name: fields
params.description: Comma-separated list of field names to include in the response. Use this to limit response payload and improve performance.

items.docs: Returns all filters defined on the specified dashboard. Dashboard filters allow users to dynamically change query parameters across all tiles that listen to a given filter.

params.name: fields
params.description: Comma-separated list of field names to include in the response. Use this to limit response payload and improve performance.

items.docs: Creates a new query object. A query defines the model, view, fields, filters, sorts, and limit used to retrieve data from the underlying database. The query is not executed until you call the run_query endpoint. Queries are immutable once created.

params.name: query_id
params.description: The unique numeric ID of the query

params.name: fields
params.description: Comma-separated list of field names to include in the response. Use this to limit response payload and improve performance.

items.docs: Retrieves a query object by its ID. Returns the query definition including the model, view, fields, filters, sorts, and limit.

params.name: query_id
params.description: The unique numeric ID of the query

params.name: result_format
params.description: Format for the query results

params.name: limit
params.description: Maximum number of results to return

params.name: apply_formatting
params.description: Whether to apply model-specified formatting

params.name: apply_vis
params.description: Whether to apply visualization options

params.name: cache
params.description: Whether to use cached results if available

params.name: generate_drill_links
params.description: Whether to generate drill links in results

params.name: force_production
params.description: Whether to force use of production models

items.docs: Executes a previously saved query and returns results in the specified format. The query must already exist. Supported formats include json, csv, txt, xlsx, html, md, json_label, json_detail, sql, and png.

params.name: result_format
params.description: Format for the query results

params.name: limit
params.description: Maximum number of results to return

params.name: apply_formatting
params.description: Whether to apply model-specified formatting

params.name: apply_vis
params.description: Whether to apply visualization options

params.name: cache
params.description: Whether to use cached results

items.docs: Creates and immediately executes a query in a single request. The query definition is provided in the request body and does not need to be saved first. This is useful for ad-hoc queries.

params.name: fields
params.description: Comma-separated list of field names to include in the response. Use this to limit response payload and improve performance.

params.name: limit
params.description: Maximum number of results to return

params.name: offset
params.description: Number of results to skip before returning results

params.name: sorts
params.description: Sort fields (e.g. last_name asc, created_at desc)

params.name: ids
params.description: Comma-separated list of user IDs to retrieve

items.docs: Returns a list of all Looker user accounts. The response includes basic user information. Use the fields parameter to include additional details such as roles, credentials, and sessions.
Creates a new Looker user account. The new user will not have any credentials or roles assigned; those must be configured separately via the credentials and role endpoints.

params.name: fields
params.description: Comma-separated list of field names to include in the response. Use this to limit response payload and improve performance.

params.name: limit
params.description: Maximum number of results to return

params.name: offset
params.description: Number of results to skip before returning results

params.name: first_name
params.description: Filter by first name

params.name: last_name
params.description: Filter by last name

params.name: email
params.description: Filter by email address

params.name: is_disabled
params.description: Filter by disabled status

params.name: sorts
params.description: Sort fields

items.docs: Searches for users matching the specified criteria. Supports filtering by name, email, and other user attributes. Results are paginated.

params.name: user_id
params.description: The unique numeric ID of the user

params.name: fields
params.description: Comma-separated list of field names to include in the response. Use this to limit response payload and improve performance.

items.docs: Retrieves a single user by their ID including their profile information, roles, and credential settings.

params.name: user_id
params.description: The unique numeric ID of the user

params.name: fields
params.description: Comma-separated list of field names to include in the response. Use this to limit response payload and improve performance.

items.docs: Updates an existing user account. Only the fields provided in the request body are updated. Credentials and roles are managed through separate endpoints.

params.name: user_id
params.description: The unique numeric ID of the user

items.docs: Permanently deletes the specified user account. This removes the user and all their credentials. Content created by the user (dashboards, Looks, etc.) is not deleted.

params.name: fields
params.description: Comma-separated list of field names to include in the response. Use this to limit response payload and improve performance.

items.docs: Returns the user record for the currently authenticated user. This is useful for determining which user is associated with the current API session.

params.name: user_id
params.description: The unique numeric ID of the user

params.name: fields
params.description: Comma-separated list of field names to include in the response. Use this to limit response payload and improve performance.

items.docs: Returns all roles assigned to the specified user. Roles define the permissions and model access granted to the user.

params.name: user_id
params.description: The unique numeric ID of the user

params.name: fields
params.description: Comma-separated list of field names to include in the response. Use this to limit response payload and improve performance.

items.docs: Replaces all roles assigned to the specified user. Provide the complete list of role IDs that should be assigned to the user. Any roles not included in the request will be removed.
